title: State of Maharashtra v. Rajesh Kumar
content: In cases involving non-bailable offences under the Bharatiya Nyaya Sanhita (BNS), bail should not be granted as a matter of right. The court must consider the gravity of the offence, the likelihood of the accused tampering with evidence, and the potential threat to witnesses. Section 480 of the Bharatiya Nagarik Suraksha Sanhita (BNSS) outlines these conditions clearly.
headnotes: Bail in non-bailable offences; Section 480 BNSS; Conditions for bail.

title: Sunitas vs State of Delhi
content: The definition of 'theft' under Section 303 of the Bharatiya Nyaya Sanhita (BNS) retains the core elements of the old IPC Section 378 but modernizes the language regarding digital assets. Dishonest intention remains the prerequisite for establishing the offence.
headnotes: Theft definition; BNS Section 303; Digital assets.
